skill.name: growth-strategy
skill.description: Plan growth strategy, GTM initiatives, experiments, activation flows, retention programs, and referral loops with clear metrics and guardrails.
skill.summary: Build structured growth plans with disciplined experimentation and ethical operating constraints.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the skill content and appear to be false positives from markdown templates and business terminology. No executable scripts, command invocation, weak cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Template Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at markdown template fences and inline reference text. These locations are documentation examples, not executable code, and there is no command runner or user input execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown fences or documentation delimiters. I found no shell execution API, script file, or instruction to run commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms, including a duplicate hit on SKILL.md line 3. The flagged lines contain skill descriptions, GTM planning text, experiment table rows, and policy measurement guidance, not cryptographic code or hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is product growth documentation. No hashing, encryption, crypto library, password handling, or security-sensitive implementation appears at these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms Used in Business Context
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance at growth strategy lines. The flagged text refers to vanity metrics, a paid growth loop, and avoiding AI content spam, with no host, network, filesystem, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plainly about growth metrics and ethical marketing constraints. I found no commands or instructions that inspect operating system, network, account, or machine state.

content.value_statement: Growth work often becomes scattered across tactics without clear metrics or guardrails. This skill turns goals into structured loops, experiments, and ethical GTM plans.
actual_capabilities: Defines north star metrics and supporting input metrics for growth work.
Structures acquisition, activation, retention, referral, and monetization initiatives.
Creates experiment plans with hypotheses, metrics, guardrails, and kill criteria.
Applies RICE and ICE scoring to prioritize growth opportunities.
Reviews tactics against privacy, deliverability, platform policy, and ethics constraints.
Guides onboarding, lifecycle, content, SEO, community, and referral growth decisions.
limitations: Does not run experiments, collect analytics data, or connect to marketing platforms.
Requires accurate product, audience, funnel, and metric context from the user.
Does not replace legal, privacy, deliverability, or platform policy review.
Provides strategy and planning guidance, not guaranteed growth results.

use_cases.title: Plan a SaaS Growth System
use_cases.description: Map a product-led growth model into loops, input metrics, activation milestones, and weekly experiment priorities.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Experiment Backlogs
use_cases.description: Convert candidate ideas into scored tests with hypotheses, sample size notes, guardrails, and kill criteria.

use_cases.title: Review Growth Tactics for Risk
use_cases.description: Check campaigns for spam, dark patterns, deceptive consent, ToS issues, and weak measurement design.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Growth Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a product or feature launch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a growth strategy for my product. Ask for missing context, then define the north star metric, key loops, activation milestone, and first five experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Experiment Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Score these growth ideas with RICE and ICE. Explain the ranking, identify missing assumptions, and recommend the next three tests.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Activation and Retention
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this onboarding and retention funnel. Identify friction, propose activation improvements, define guardrails, and write experiment briefs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Experiment System
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a growth experimentation operating system for my team. Include intake rules, scoring, weekly cadence, documentation standards, measurement constraints, and ethical red lines.

output_examples.input: We need more trial users to reach activation in a B2B SaaS product.
output: Defines activation as reaching a first meaningful outcome, not completing signup.
Proposes tests for templates, guided setup, import flows, and first-value messaging.
Adds guardrails for churn, support tickets, spam complaints, and refund risk.

output_examples.input: Our team has 20 growth ideas and no prioritization process.
output: Creates a scored backlog using reach, impact, confidence, effort, and ease.
Separates quick learning tests from larger system changes.
Recommends a weekly review cadence with kill criteria and learning notes.

output_examples.input: We want to launch a referral program for existing customers.
output: Frames the referral loop around activated customers and shareable value.
Compares give-get incentives, placement options, and fraud guardrails.
Defines success metrics for referral activation, retention, and payback.

best_practices: Start with a clear metric model before choosing channels or tactics.
Document every experiment with a hypothesis, target segment, guardrails, and kill criteria.
Review privacy, deliverability, consent, and platform rules before launch.
anti_patterns: Optimizing vanity metrics that do not connect to retention or revenue.
Shipping growth tactics without measurement, guardrails, or learning goals.
Using spam, fake reviews, dark patterns, or scraping that violates terms.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create growth strategies, GTM plans, experiment briefs, prioritization systems, and ethical growth reviews.

faq.question: Can it analyze my live analytics data?
faq.answer: No. It can reason from data you provide, but it does not connect to analytics, CRM, or advertising tools.

faq.question: Does it support A/B test planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines hypotheses, segments, metrics, sample size considerations, guardrails, duration, and kill criteria.

faq.question: Is this only for SaaS companies?
faq.answer: No. It is strongest for product-led and B2B SaaS contexts, but the planning framework can support many digital products.

faq.question: How does it handle risky growth tactics?
faq.answer: It flags spam, fake reviews, dark patterns, deceptive consent, ToS issues, and privacy concerns as red lines.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide your product, audience, funnel metrics, current channels, constraints, and candidate growth ideas.
